title: CI: completion system test: use safename
body:
Ongoing efforts to make system tests parallel-safe

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Don't see this as being safer, but more consistent.
/lgtm

>Don't see this as being safer, but more consistent.

Good point. Excising the `rmi` is safer in terms of parallelization. The `$(safename)` serves two purposes, one being safety similar to `random_string`, the other is for the `leak_check` at the end of a run. Seeing `Leaked volume v-t123-sdfsdf` makes it easy for a human to track down `test 123` and look for volume leaks there. Full context is in #23275.

Anyone know what's going on with CI? Why it's stuck here and in #23275?

> Anyone know what's going on with CI? Why it's stuck here and in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23275?

I don't know try to force push and see if that helps.

> > Anyone know what's going on with CI? Why it's stuck here and in #23275?
> 
> I don't know try to force push and see if that helps.

The macos worker pool is down, thus all `Build for MacOS amd64+arm64` cannot run all task depending on that are blocked. I pinged @cevich about it.
